I couldn't find any information on the plant "Thogsennia lindeniana." However, I can provide information on a similar plant called "Thogsennia lindheimeriana",  but I'll give you information on Lindheimer's oak (Quercus lindheimeriana) and Quercus lindeniana is a synonym of this. 

Lindheimer's oak (Quercus lindheimeriana) is a deciduous tree species native to Texas and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Lindheimer's oak include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lindheimer's oak include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
